trailWOMAroot1.^description: No alarm found for usage of 'root' user. The root user has unrestricted access to all services and resources in an AWS account. We highly recommend that you avoid using the root user for daily tasks. Minimizing the use of the root user and adopting the principle of least privilege for access management reduce the risk of accidental changes and unintended disclosure of highly privileged credentials.

trailWOMAunauthAPI2.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm unauthorized API calls. Monitoring unauthorized API calls helps reveal application errors and might reduce time to detect malicious activity.

trailWOMAnoMFA3.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm console logins that aren't protected by MFA. Monitoring for single-factor console logins increases visibility into accounts that aren't protected by MFA.
trailWOMAnoMFA3.shortDesc: Create alarm: Console Sign-in without MFA

trailWOMAalarm4.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for changes made to IAM policies. Monitoring these changes helps ensure that authentication and authorization controls remain intact.

trailWOMATrail5.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for changes to CloudTrail configuration settings. Monitoring these changes helps ensure sustained visibility to activities in the account.

trailWOMAAuthFail6.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for failed console authentication attempts. Monitoring failed console logins might decrease lead time to detect an attempt to brute-force a credential, which might provide an indicator, such as source IP, that you can use in other event correlations.

trailWOMACMK7.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for customer managed keys that have changed state to disabled or scheduled deletion. Data encrypted with disabled or deleted keys is no longer accessible.

trailWOMAS3Policy8.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for changes to S3 bucket policies. Monitoring these changes might reduce time to detect and correct permissive policies on sensitive S3 buckets.
trailWOMAS3Policy8.shortDesc: Create alarm: S3 Bucket Policy changes

trailWOMAConfig9.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for changes to AWS Config configuration settings. Monitoring these changes helps ensure sustained visibility of configuration items in the account.

trailWOMASecGroup10.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for changes to security groups. Monitoring these changes helps ensure that resources and services aren't unintentionally exposed.

trailWOMANACL11.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for changes to NACLs. Monitoring these changes helps ensure that AWS resources and services aren't unintentionally exposed.

trailWOMAGateway12.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for changes to network gateways. Monitoring these changes helps ensure that all ingress and egress traffic traverses the VPC border via a controlled path.

trailWOMARouteTable13.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for changes to route tables. Monitoring these changes helps ensure that all VPC traffic flows through an expected path.

trailWOMAVPC14.^description: CIS recommends that you create a metric filter and alarm for changes to VPCs. Monitoring these changes helps ensure that authentication and authorization controls remain intact.

trailWithoutCWLogs.^description: CIS recommends that all CloudTrail to have logs store in CloudWatch so relevant CloudWatch log metrics control can be implement to heighten both AWS Security Pillar and Operation Excellence Pillar

trailWithCWLogsWithoutMetrics.^description: No metrics found in CloudWatch Log Group. CIS recommends that you create relevant metric filters and alarms for CloudTrail's log that store in CloudWatch Log
trailWithCWLogsWithoutMetrics.shortDesc: CloudTrail's Log to have log metrics

SetRetentionDays.^description: No retention day set in CloudWatch Log Groups. It incurs charges based on storage size

CISRetentionAtLeast1Yr.^description: CloudWatch Logs centralize logs from all of your systems, applications, and AWS services in a single, highly scalable service. You can use CloudWatch Logs to monitor, store, and access your log files from Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) instances, AWS CloudTrail, Amazon Route 53, and other sources. Retaining your logs for at least 1 year can help you comply with log retention standards.
CISRetentionAtLeast1Yr.shortDesc: To have at least 365 days retention

alarmsWithoutSNS.^description: CloudWatch alarms without SNS topic actions configured provide no value for operational awareness and incident response. Alarms must have SNS notifications configured to alert teams when thresholds are breached. Without notifications, alarms cannot trigger automated responses or notify on-call personnel, defeating their purpose as a monitoring tool.

missingBillingAlarms.^description: Billing alarms prevent unexpected cost overruns and provide early warning of cost anomalies. Without billing alarms configured, organizations risk unexpected charges and lack visibility into spending patterns. Billing alarms should be configured to monitor AWS costs and alert when spending exceeds defined thresholds. Note that billing metrics are only available in the us-east-1 region.

missingServiceQuotaAlarms.^description: Service quotas are critical limits that can cause service disruptions if exceeded. Alarms should monitor AWS/Usage namespace metrics for critical services (VPC, EC2, Lambda, RDS, ECS) to provide early warning before hitting quota limits. Without service quota alarms, you risk unexpected service failures when quotas are reached.

cloudwatchResourcesWithoutTags.^description: CloudWatch resources (log groups, alarms) should have required cost allocation tags (Environment, Project, CostCenter) for proper cost tracking and resource management. Without proper tagging, it becomes difficult to allocate costs, identify resource ownership, and manage resources at scale.
cloudwatchResourcesWithoutTags.shortDesc: Add required tags to CloudWatch resources

logGroupsWithoutLogInsightsUsage.^description: CloudWatch Logs Insights provides powerful query capabilities for analyzing log data. Log groups without recent query activity (30 days) may indicate underutilization of available analysis tools. Regular use of Logs Insights helps identify patterns, troubleshoot issues, and gain operational insights from log data.
logGroupsWithoutLogInsightsUsage.shortDesc: Use Log Insights for log analysis

missingCrossAccountDashboards.^description: Cross-account CloudWatch dashboards provide centralized visibility across multiple AWS accounts, essential for multi-account environments. Dashboards without cross-account metrics miss opportunities for unified monitoring and operational awareness across your organization.

alarmsWithoutAutoScalingActions.^description: Alarms monitoring Auto Scaling-related metrics (AWS/EC2, AWS/ECS, AWS/ApplicationAutoScaling) should trigger Auto Scaling policies to automatically adjust capacity. Without Auto Scaling actions, alarms provide alerts but miss opportunities for automatic remediation and cost optimization.
alarmsWithoutAutoScalingActions.shortDesc: Add Auto Scaling actions to alarms

missingApplicationSignals.^description: CloudWatch Application Signals provides automatic application topology discovery, service level objective (SLO) monitoring, and dependency mapping for microservices. Without Application Signals, you lack visibility into application health, service dependencies, and SLO compliance.

missingXRayIntegration.^description: AWS X-Ray provides distributed tracing for microservices, enabling performance bottleneck identification, service dependency mapping, and request flow visualization. Without X-Ray sampling rules configured, you lack detailed tracing capabilities for troubleshooting and performance optimization.

missingCloudWatchDashboards.^description: CloudWatch dashboards provide customizable visualization of metrics and logs, essential for operational awareness and monitoring. Without dashboards configured, teams lack centralized views of system health and performance metrics.

missingCustomMetrics.^description: Custom metrics enable monitoring of application-specific KPIs and business metrics beyond AWS-provided metrics. Without custom metrics, you lack visibility into application behavior, business metrics, and custom error rates that are critical for application monitoring.

alarmsWithoutMetricMath.^description: Metric math allows combining multiple metrics using mathematical expressions, enabling more sophisticated monitoring scenarios. Alarms without metric math may miss opportunities for advanced monitoring patterns like rate calculations, anomaly detection, and multi-metric correlations.

missingCompositeAlarms.^description: Composite alarms combine multiple alarms using boolean logic (AND, OR, NOT) to reduce alarm noise and create higher-level monitoring conditions. Without composite alarms, you may experience alarm fatigue from too many individual alarms and miss complex failure scenarios that require multiple conditions.

failedScheduledQueries.^description: Failed or cancelled CloudWatch Logs Insights scheduled queries indicate issues with automated log analysis. Query failures can result from permission issues, syntax errors, or resource constraints, preventing automated monitoring and alerting from functioning properly.

missingVendedDashboards.^description: Vended dashboards are pre-built CloudWatch dashboards automatically generated for specific log types (VPC Flow Logs, CloudTrail, WAF). These dashboards provide immediate visualization value without manual configuration. Missing vended dashboards represents missed opportunities for quick operational insights.
